Title: The Mediator complex subunit MED25 interacts with HDA9 and PIF4 to regulate thermomorphogenesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mediator complex subunit 25; contains vWF-A, MD, ACID, and glutamine-rich GD/polyQ domains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full warm-temperature induction of YUCCA8 and proper hypocotyl elongation; also represses PIF4 expression and destabilizes HDA9 protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. med25/pft1 mutants showed reduced hypocotyl elongation at warm temperature, indicating MED25 is required for thermomorphogenesis. 2. MED25 represses PIF4 promoter activity and endogenous PIF4 transcript accumulation. 3. MED25 promotes warm-temperature induction of YUCCA8, associates more strongly with PIF4 and YUCCA8 loci at high temperature, interacts with both PIF4 and HDA9, and negatively affects HDA9 protein stability.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional effects detected in seedlings, particularly under warm temperature and toward the end of the day
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter; native PIF4 promoter reporter; native YUCCA8 promoter reporter
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Associates with chromatin at PIF4 and YUCCA8 loci; proposed to recruit histone deacetylation machinery via HDA9 at YUCCA8
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants (pft1-2, pft1-3); overexpression; pPIF4:LUC and pYUCCA8:LUC assays; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; BiFC; split-LUC; co-immunoprecipitation; HDA9-LUC stability assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Both loss and overexpression reduce warm-temperature hypocotyl elongation, indicating dosage sensitivity and possible pleiotropy due to Mediator-related functions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to auxin-related thermomorphogenesis, MED25 is not an enzyme or canonical TF; it likely acts as a scaffold linking PIF4 and HDA9 at YUCCA8 while repressing PIF4 transcription in parallel

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key induced auxin biosynthetic gene during thermomorphogenesis, but rate-limiting status not directly established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: stimulates elongation growth during warm-temperature responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Warm-temperature induction of YUCCA8 is reduced in med25/pft1 mutant, linking diminished auxin biosynthesis capacity to impaired hypocotyl elongation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. YUCCA8 expression is strongly induced by warm temperature in WT. 2. This induction is limited in the med25/pft1 mutant despite elevated PIF4 transcript levels. 3. MED25 shows increased association with the YUCCA8 locus at warm temperature, supporting a causal role in YUCCA8 activation during thermomorphogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in 2-week-old seedlings at 27°C, measured at ZT8
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: YUCCA8 activation under warmth depends on HDA9-mediated histone deacetylation/H2A.Z depletion as discussed and on MED25 association to the locus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of endogenous YUCCA8; transient pYUCCA8:LUC reporter assays; MED25 ChIP-qPCR at YUCCA8 locus
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on YUCCA8 transcriptional control rather than direct enzymology or metabolite quantification

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes warm-temperature elongation through auxin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin biosynthesis gene activation during thermomorphogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of auxin biosynthetic genes; autoregulatory/self-promoter-associated expression is discussed; activity depends on MED25 at YUCCA8
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Warm temperature; diurnal cues; phytochrome-related signaling
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: PIF4 can bind its own promoter, suggesting autoregulatory control discussed in the paper
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Associates with MED25; no direct interaction with HDA9 detected
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major warm-temperature transcriptional activator linking heat signals to auxin biosynthesis and elongation growth
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression peaks toward the end of the day; higher at warm temperature
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in pft1-2 than WT at both 22°C and 27°C; diurnal expression increases toward dusk
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native PIF4 promoter reporter; 35S constitutive promoter in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: PIF4 expression is discussed in relation to histone deacetylation/acetylation at its locus, though MED25–HDA9 control at PIF4 appears HDA9-independent
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pPIF4:LUC stable reporter; RT-qPCR; transient promoter assay in N. benthamiana; BiFC; split-LUC; co-immunoprecipitation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated PIF4 transcript alone is insufficient to drive elongation in med25 mutant background, indicating context dependence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED25 suppresses PIF4 promoter activity and endogenous PIF4 transcript accumulation. 2. PIF4 physically interacts with MED25 in the nucleus. 3. Despite elevated PIF4 expression in med25 mutant, YUCCA8 induction and hypocotyl elongation are impaired, showing that PIF4 activity requires MED25 for full output.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the key transcriptional activator of YUCCA8/auxin biosynthesis, but its effective output during thermomorphogenesis depends on MED25

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports auxin-mediated elongation during high temperature adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Epigenetic activation of auxin biosynthesis gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Histone deacetylation and chromatin remodeling; H2A.Z depletion at YUCCA8 locus discussed
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes YUCCA8 transcription indirectly by enabling permissive chromatin state
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with MED25; does not directly interact with PIF4
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential chromatin regulator required for full warm-temperature YUCCA8 induction and elongation response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Protein stability/activity influenced by temperature, light/dark, and proteasome activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: HDA9-LUC activity rises strongly at night and is modulated by temperature and MED25 background
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Warm temperature (27–28°C); light/dark cycle; MG132 proteasome inhibitor
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Functions as an epigenetic regulator mediating histone deacetylation and facilitating H2A.Z eviction at YUCCA8; protein abundance is controlled by proteasome activity and antagonized by MED25
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic interaction in pft1-2 hda9-1 double mutant; Y2H; BiFC; split-LUC; HDA9-LUC reporter assays; eGFP-HDA9 immunoblot with/without MG132
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Protein destabilization by high MED25 levels may reduce thermomorphogenic growth; regulation is highly context dependent
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hda9 mutation does not add to the med25 mutant hypocotyl defect, consistent with a shared pathway. 2. HDA9 interacts with MED25 but not detectably with PIF4. 3. MED25 antagonizes HDA9 protein accumulation/stability, while HDA9 supports YUCCA8 activation required for thermomorphogenesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes thermomorphogenesis indirectly by enabling YUCCA8 expression through chromatin remodeling, with its protein stability negatively regulated by MED25
